verdict_reasoning: VEGFA is a classically secreted growth factor with a signal peptide but no transmembrane domain or GPI anchor. Its cell-surface association — seen for VEGF165 and VEGF189 isoforms — is mediated by reversible, non-covalent binding to heparan sulfate proteoglycans (HSPGs) on the extracellular matrix and cell surface, readily reversed by heparin, heparinase, or plasmin. This fails the wash-resistance criterion for 'stable_surface_attachment'. Pre-no checklist: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented outer-leaflet translocation of the VEGFA protein body itself under stress, ICD, or oncogenic state; hypoxia upregulates expression but the protein remains secreted; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no isoform carries its own TM or GPI anchor in any lineage; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — VEGFA is not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization — cytoplasmic/Golgi and secreted pools exist but no bona fide PM-anchored minority pool; HSPG binding is reversible; (5) stable_surface_attachment — HSPG binding is heparin-releasable and therefore not wash-resistant. All approved anti-VEGFA therapeutics (bevacizumab, ranibizumab, aflibercept) target the circulating soluble form, not a cell-surface form.
